sentence_reviews.text: API gateways and tool registries authenticate requests, describe callable capabilities, validate arguments, enforce policy, and route agents to external services.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports service/data-system routing and operations broadly; structured-output and NIST references support argument schemas and governance, but not this exact gateway/tool-registry definition.
sentence_reviews.notes: Definition is defensible from multiple sources, but a direct API gateway or agent tool-use source would be better.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Caches, APIs & tool interfaces
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports caches and service interfaces generally, but not AI tool gateways specifically.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Caches, APIs & tool interfaces, External databases, APIs & live tools.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors is modeled as depending on Caches, APIs & tool interfaces, External databases, APIs & live tools and feeding downstream nodes such as Conversational assistants & chatbots.
